description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese torii gate, typically found at the entrance to Shinto shrines. It features two tall, cylindrical red pillars supporting a pair of horizontal crossbars, with the uppermost crossbar slightly curved and painted black. The structure is simple yet iconic, with clean lines and a striking color contrast between the red pillars and the black top beam. The base of each pillar is slightly flared and darkened, suggesting stone or protective coverings. The open space beneath the crossbars allows for passage, symbolizing the transition from the mundane to the sacred. The overall design is minimalistic, with no visible ornamentation or inscriptions.
description: A traditional Japanese torii gate with red pillars and a curved black top beam, symbolizing the entrance to a sacred space.
description_view_0: Front view showing both red pillars, the horizontal crossbars, and the curved black top beam.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile of the gate and the thickness of the top beam.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the same structural elements.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the curvature of the top beam.
